metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/README.md` to the scope and to the AC (or add an AC bullet) requiring that both new docs are linked from the `docs/README.md` index, so the `test_current_repo_index_is_clean` gate passes.
evidence: tests/unit/test_check_docs_index.py line 44-46: `def test_current_repo_index_is_clean(): assert chk.main(['--check']) == 0`
test_check_docs_index.py line 67-79: 'A living top-level docs/*.md not linked from the index is reported' — test_unindexed_living_doc_is_flagged
Scope lists: 'docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md (new), docs/passing-code-review.md (new)' but no docs/README.md
docs/README.md already has an entry for plan-review-criteria-guide.md but no entry for the two new files (absence confirmed by directory listing and search)
findings.finding: The plan adds two new top-level docs files (`docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md` and `docs/passing-code-review.md`) but does not include `docs/README.md` in scope or in the ACs. A live CI gate (`test_check_docs_index.py::test_current_repo_index_is_clean`) enforces that every top-level `docs/*.md` is linked from `docs/README.md` — adding the two new files without updating the index will break this test.
findings.location: Scope section (no docs/README.md entry) and AC bullets (no index-update requirement)
scenarios: CI runs `pytest tests/unit/test_check_docs_index.py::test_current_repo_index_is_clean` after the new files land — it will find two unindexed docs and return non-zero, breaking the Verified gate.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `docs/README.md` to the Scope file list and an AC line: '`docs/README.md` links both new docs so `test_current_repo_index_is_clean` passes (the docs-index completeness gate).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/README.md` to the Scope as an edit target, with entries for both `docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md` and `docs/passing-code-review.md` under the appropriate audience section (Contributor or Agent).
evidence: tests/unit/test_check_docs_index.py line 44: `def test_current_repo_index_is_clean()` — 'The real, committed docs/README.md passes both checks (exit 0)'
test_check_docs_index.py docstring: 'every living top-level docs/*.md must be linked from the index'
docs/README.md is not listed in the Scope section as an edit target
AGENTS.md itself says 'The docs/ index is docs/README.md'
findings.finding: `docs/README.md` is an unenumerated consumer: an existing CI test (`tests/unit/test_check_docs_index.py::test_current_repo_index_is_clean`) enforces that every top-level `docs/*.md` file is linked from `docs/README.md`. Adding `docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md` and `docs/passing-code-review.md` without also adding entries in `docs/README.md` will break this test.
scenarios: Developer creates both new docs files but does not add them to docs/README.md; `make lint` / `pytest tests/unit/test_check_docs_index.py` fails the CI `Verified` gate with 'missing from index' errors.
AC5 says '`make lint` and the explain unit tests pass' — the docs-index test is part of the unit test suite and would be violated.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `docs/README.md` to the Scope list and include a note that both new docs files must be linked there under an appropriate audience heading (e.g. Agent or Contributor), so the `test_current_repo_index_is_clean` CI gate continues to pass.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete content-verification step to AC1 and AC2 — e.g. `grep -q 'description template' docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md` and `grep -q 'blocking-criteria' docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md` (and analogous checks for AC2's required sections) — so the content requirements are machine-verifiable, not just inferred from 'reading the file'.
evidence: AC1: 'exists and states the description template + the blocking-criteria checklist; verified by reading the file and rebar explain plan printing it (exit 0)'
AC2: 'exists covering the commit checklist, LLM-Review dimensions (blocking vs advisory), and vote responses; rebar explain review prints it (exit 0)'
ABSENCE: no grep/search command is specified to assert the required section headings or keywords are present in the written files
findings.finding: AC1 and AC2 state that the docs must CONTAIN specific content (description template + blocking-criteria checklist; commit checklist + LLM-Review dimensions + vote responses), but the only proving checks are file existence and `rebar explain <name>` exit 0 — neither check verifies the required content sections are actually present in the file.
scenarios: An agent creates a stub file with placeholder text; `rebar explain plan` exits 0 (the file exists and is readable) but the required description template and checklist are absent — AC1 passes mechanically while its content requirement is unmet.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC1: 'and `grep -qE 'description template|blocking.criter' docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md` exits 0'. Append to AC2: 'and `grep -qE 'commit checklist|blocking.*advisory|vote responses' docs/passing-code-review.md` exits 0'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC 3) to assert that `rebar explain <guide-name>` exits 1 with a structured `missing-file` error when a registered guide's file is absent.
evidence: Testing: 'success for every registered guide, unknown-name → `unknown-id`, missing-file → `missing-file`'
ABSENCE: No AC references 'missing-file' or the file-absent error path
findings.finding: The Testing section specifies a `missing-file` error path ('missing-file' result when the registered guide file is absent) but no Acceptance Criterion verifies this behavior, leaving it as described-but-uncovered work.
findings.location: Testing section ('success for every registered guide, unknown-name → `unknown-id`, missing-file → `missing-file`') vs. Acceptance Criteria (AC 1–5)
scenarios: An implementer writes the missing-file error path as specified in Testing, but the gate has no AC to verify it — the behavior could be silently removed or broken without failing any acceptance check.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC 3: '…; a registered guide whose file is missing exits 1 with a structured `missing-file` error.' This mirrors the Testing section and closes the coverage gap.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the `argparse` description and positional-arg help-text update for `_explain` in the Scope (or confirm it is deliberately deferred), so the help text reflects that both criterion IDs and guide names (`plan`, `review`) are accepted.
evidence: _llm_commands.py line 395: description='Print the plan-review criteria authoring-guide section for a criterion id'
_llm_commands.py line 397: `parser.add_argument('criterion_id', nargs='?', help='a plan-review criterion id (e.g. F1, G3)')`
Plan only says '_explain routes guide names' without enumerating the argparse text update
AC3: 'an unknown topic exits 1 with a structured unknown-id error' — implies `plan`/`review` must be valid inputs alongside criterion IDs
findings.finding: The `_explain` CLI function's argparse description and positional argument name are both keyed to criterion IDs ('a plan-review criterion id (e.g. F1, G3)' / arg named `criterion_id`). After routing guide names (`plan`, `review`), these will be stale/misleading — but updating them is not mentioned in the Scope or plan prose.
scenarios: After implementation, `rebar explain --help` still says 'criterion id (e.g. F1, G3)' — a user running `rebar explain plan` sees no hint in the help that guide names are valid.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the positional arg name to `topic` (or `name`) and update the help string to 'a plan-review criterion id (e.g. F1) or a guide name (plan, review)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the `explain_criterion` MCP tool docstring (and optionally parameter name) is updated to advertise that `"plan"` and `"review"` are valid guide-name inputs alongside criterion IDs.
evidence: Current docstring: 'Explain a plan-review criterion — its authoring-guide section ... On failure returns a structured error {error, kind} (kind ∈ unknown-id / malformed-registry / missing-file).'
Plan says '_mcp_reads.py (explain_criterion routes guide names)' but does not specify updating the tool's description or noting the new accepted inputs.
MCP tool descriptions are the primary discovery surface for MCP clients; an undocumented input defeats the discoverability goal the plan is solving.
findings.finding: The MCP `explain_criterion` tool's docstring and parameter name (`criterion_id`) will be stale after the change: it currently says 'Explain a plan-review criterion' and lists 'kind ∈ unknown-id / malformed-registry / missing-file', but after routing guide names through it the tool also serves author guides — an MCP consumer reading the tool description will not know `"plan"` and `"review"` are valid inputs, defeating part of the discoverability goal.
findings.location: src/rebar/_mcp_reads.py — explain_criterion tool docstring; Scope section lists _mcp_reads.py
scenarios: An agent or contributor queries the MCP tool list, reads 'Explain a plan-review criterion', and never discovers that `explain_criterion "plan"` returns the author on-ramp doc.
An MCP client that validates inputs against the tool description may reject `"plan"` as an unexpected value for `criterion_id`.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope entry for `_mcp_reads.py`, add: update the `explain_criterion` tool docstring to note that the accepted input is `criterion_id | guide_name` and that `"plan"` / `"review"` return the author on-ramp guides.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify WHERE the guide-vs-criterion dispatch lives (e.g. a unified `explain_topic(name)` in registry.py that checks `AUTHOR_GUIDES` first, then `explain_criterion`), and document the precedence rule for any name collision between a guide key and a criterion id.
evidence: Plan says '_explain routes guide names' and 'explain_criterion routes guide names' but gives no dispatch pseudocode or decision rule
The existing _explain function (lines 384-406 of _llm_commands.py) calls only registry.explain_criterion — no branching exists yet
The existing MCP explain_criterion tool (lines 53-64 of _mcp_reads.py) calls only registry.explain_criterion — no branching exists yet
Test spec says 'success for every registered guide, unknown-name → unknown-id' without naming which function is called, leaving the seam boundary ambiguous

AUTHOR_GUIDES key vocabulary ('plan', 'review') is referenced implicitly but never enumerated; an implementer cannot verify from the plan whether 'rebar explain plan-review' would route to explain_guide or explain_criterion
findings.finding: The dispatch rule between the new `explain_guide` and the existing `explain_criterion` across all three surfaces (CLI `_explain`, MCP `explain_criterion`, library) is never specified in the plan. The plan says each surface 'routes guide names' to `explain_guide` and criterion ids to `explain_criterion`, but never defines WHERE the branching logic lives (in the registry as a unified lookup? in each caller independently? via a prefix check?), making the protocol ambiguous for an implementer.
findings.location: Scope section: 'src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py (AUTHOR_GUIDES + explain_guide)' / 'src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py (_explain routes guide names)' / 'src/rebar/_mcp_reads.py (explain_criterion routes guide names)'
scenarios: An implementer adds a try/except fallback in _explain (try explain_guide first, then explain_criterion) — unknown guide names then silently fall through to unknown-criterion errors with a confusing message
An implementer adds the routing to the registry as a unified lookup — MCP tool name explain_criterion no longer accurately describes its scope, requiring docstring update not mentioned in the plan
A future criterion is named 'plan' or 'review' — the precedence rule is undefined
findings.suggested_fix: Add a paragraph to the Approach section: 'A new `explain_topic(name)` function in `registry.py` serves as the single dispatch point: it checks `AUTHOR_GUIDES` first (returning the guide's file content if matched), then delegates to `explain_criterion` for criterion ids. The CLI `_explain` and MCP `explain_criterion` both call `explain_topic`. Unknown names raise `ExplainError("unknown-id", ...)` listing both registered guide names and known criterion ids.' The MCP tool rename (or docstring update) should also be enumerated in Scope.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
